answer:
\boxed{B}

Glucocorticoids (e.g., prednisone) are well-known to cause hyperglycemia by increasing gluconeogenesis, reducing glucose uptake in peripheral tissues, and impairing insulin sensitivity. While β-blockers can mask hypoglycemia symptoms, they do not directly cause hyperglycemia. Acetylcholine and α-blockers are not typically associated with this metabolic effect.
